threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus stop is called 'the bus stop' instead of 'the pub by the corner shop.'
Last night on the 35, a guy sang the entire 'Bohemian Rhapsody' to a shocked couple. They didn't even applaud when he finished.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy in a tracksuit arguing passionately with a pigeon outside Reuterplatz, I knew I was home. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar where they serve cocktails in old cassette tapes. It’s a blast from the past with a twist!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they get annoyed at tourists staring at their phones, oblivious to the BART shutdown announcements.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: The Muni bus I was on stopped suddenly today because a man tried to skateboard through the double doors. Classic Tenderloin chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the best time to visit Plaça del Sol is at night. But the real magic happens in the morning when the locals grab coffee.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they hesitate at intersections, unsure if they should wait for the light or just cross—locals know the chaos is part of the charm.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus at the wrong stop, looking confused when everyone else knows to skip that one on a weekday.
Last night on the 53, a guy serenaded the driver with a ukulele. Must've been at least three stops before someone joined in.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was living in Neukölln when I saw someone using a broken shopping cart as a stroller at the park. It felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a techno club that doubles as a bakery by day, serving pastries that are just as experimental as the music.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local when they know the best taco spots have no lines after 10 PM, while the newcomers flock to the tourist traps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, a bus driver stopped mid-route to help a lost tourist find their way—only in SF will you see kindness amidst chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the hipster cafés, but the real vibe is in the corners where locals still play cards and chat over a caña.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they hesitate at crosswalks, waiting for the green light, while locals stroll straight through.
